content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes two numbers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input and performs the corresponding operation using a switch statement. Print the result upto 2 decimal places.
constraints: Division by zero will never occur
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two space‑separated numbers num1 and num2 (both may be real numbers). The second line contains a single character op which is one of '+', '-', '*', '/'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the result of applying the operator to num1 and num2, rounded and displayed with exactly two digits after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. 1 + 2 = 3, printed with two decimal places.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each case read the two operands and the operator. Using a switch statement compute the required arithmetic operation. Because the operands can be non‑integers we store them in double variables. The result is printed with two decimal places using formatted output (Locale.US to force '.' as decimal separator). The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few primitive variables and the output buffer are used.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());double num1=Double.parseDouble(st.nextToken());double num2=Double.parseDouble(st.nextToken());char op=br.readLine().trim().charAt(0);double result;switch(op){case '+':result=num1+num2;break;case '-':result=num1-num2;break;case '*':result=num1*num2;break;case '/':result=num1/num2;break;default:result=0;break;}sb.append(String.format(Locale.US,"%.2f",result)).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
